page.content: As Enel SpA continues to advance its renewable energy and utility initiatives, the progress of grid interconnection and smart meter rollout has become increasingly critical.

lines.text: As Enel SpA continues to advance its renewable energy and utility initiatives, the progress of grid interconnection and smart
meter rollout has become increasingly critical.

page.content: The current data reflects a total capacity in the grid interconnection queue of CHF 300 million, highlighting significant investment in renewable energy infrastructure. The metrics indicate that there are currently 12 active projects underway, with an additional 10 projects pending approval. This progress is vital, especially as Europe strives to achieve its ambitious sustainability targets. Notably, the rollout of smart meters has seen a positive trend, with a reported completion rate of 85% in active regions.

page.content: Comparatively, North America has also made substantial strides in this area, with a pending project count of 5 and an impressive active project figure of 25. In line with these efforts, last year’s performance indicated a total of 150,000 smart meters installed, which corresponds to a 25% increase from the previous year. The overall outlook remains optimistic, with expectations for a further 3,000,000 capacity in North America, projected at 90% completion by the second quarter of FY2025. These metrics underscore Enel SpA’s commitment to enhancing grid reliability and expanding renewable energy resources across the continent.
